question_title: Will KMB's market close price on 2026-04-25 be higher than its market close price on 2026-04-15?
summary: KMB is in a sustained downtrend with ~33% drawdown from its 52-week high, negative daily drift over both 20-day (-0.16%/day) and 6-month (-0.13%/day) windows. Monte Carlo simulations using empirical drift give P(higher) of 38-41%, while zero-drift gives ~49%. The deep drawdown conditional base rate (76.5% positive, n=17) supports mean reversion, but the sample is small and some bounce from the April 7 low ($93.05) has already occurred. The broader market is rallying strongly (S&P 500 near highs), providing some tailwind, but KMB faces company-specific headwinds: multiple analyst downgrades (Wells Fargo $100, UBS $105, Barclays $99) and inflation-driven margin concerns. Q1 earnings are April 28 (after the window), so no major catalyst exists. Balancing negative drift against market support and near-52w-low dynamics, I estimate slightly below 50%.
reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.46 (46% probability that KMB closes higher on April 25 than April 15)**

Key reasoning:
- **Negative drift dominates**: KMB's trailing 20-day mean return of -0.16%/day and 6-month mean of -0.13%/day suggest continuation of the downtrend. Monte Carlo simulations with these parameters give 38-41% chance of being higher.
- **Deep drawdown offers some support**: At ~33% below 52-week highs, historical conditional returns are positive 76.5% of the time over 8 days, but this is based on only 17 events and some mean reversion has already occurred (bounced +4.4% from the April 7 low).
- **Market tailwind vs company headwinds**: S&P 500 is rallying strongly near 52w highs, but KMB faces analyst downgrades and inflation/margin concerns.
- **No catalyst in window**: Q1 earnings aren't until April 28, leaving no major scheduled event to drive directional movement.
sources_consulted: [What to Expect From Kimberly-Clark's Q1 2026 Earnings Report](https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/article/barchart-2026-4-6-what-to-expect-from-kimberly-clarks-q1-2026-earnings-report)
resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the latest market close price of KMB. If it is higher than the close price on 2026-04-15, the question will resolve to 'Yes'. The close price will be pulled from the Yahoo Finance API.
fine_print: Stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for in resolving this question. Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price.
